hero:
# Crime Risk Modeling

**Socio-economic crime-rate modeling · Python · Pandas · Scikit-learn**

## Hero Overview

A regression model forecasting regional crime rates from socio-economic indicators, with engineered interaction terms and PCA isolating the 6 variables driving regional fluctuation.

Outputs were distilled into variable-level summaries to anchor socio-economic policy briefs on quantitative evidence.

points.evidence: Cleaned and ran EDA on 10K+ crime records, surfacing correlations across unemployment, education and income.
Engineered log transforms and 2 interaction terms capturing financial-instability and education-disparity effects (+10% accuracy).
Multivariate Linear Regression forecasting regional crime at ~78% accuracy; PCA extracted 6 key driving variables.
Distilled regression outputs into 6-variable summaries anchoring socio-economic policy briefs for state authorities.

network_graph.title: Technology Capability Network
network_graph.description: Maps the project's core techniques to the tools used to implement them.

storyline:
# Storyline

## Context

Regional crime patterns track socio-economic conditions, but policy conversations needed a defensible, quantitative link rather than anecdote.

## Workflow

EDA on socio-economic indicators → interaction-term feature engineering → regression modeling → PCA variable extraction → policy briefs.

## Technical Implementation

- **Analysis:** 10K+ crime records with Pandas/NumPy, correlating unemployment, education and income.
- **Features:** log transforms and 2 interaction terms (+10% accuracy) capturing compound effects.
- **Modeling:** Multivariate Linear Regression at ~78% accuracy; PCA isolated 6 key driving variables.

## Outcomes

Distilled outputs into 6-variable summaries state authorities could act on, anchoring socio-economic policy briefs on evidence.
